concerns.text: Traceability sections refer to “US-1” (e.g., “(See US-1)”), but the document labels the story as “User Story 1”. No identifier US-1 is defined elsewhere, causing a mismatch between the referenced ID and the actual story label.

Similarly, User Story 2’s traceability cites “(See US-1)”. This appears to be a copy‑paste error; the correct reference should be “US-2” or “User Story 2”. The inconsistent identifier breaks traceability consistency.
User Story 3 also uses “(See US-1)” in its traceability block, which does not correspond to any defined story identifier, further propagating the same inconsistency.

The specification assumes that computing ~200 M pairwise correlations for ~20 k genes on a GitHub Actions runner with only 2 CPU cores and 7 GB RAM will finish within the 6‑hour wall‑clock budget (FR‑004, FR‑009, and the Assumptions section). Even with block‑wise streaming, the required ~10 B arithmetic operations are unlikely to complete in this environment, making the core analysis infeasible and jeopardizing the ability to generate the required evaluation metrics.

responses.response: Added explicit user story identifier US-1 to User Story 1 and updated its traceability reference to (See US-1).
responses.what_changed: Modified User Story 1 heading and traceability block to use US-1 identifier.

responses.response: Added explicit user story identifier US-2 to User Story 2 and corrected its traceability reference to (See US-2).
responses.what_changed: Modified User Story 2 heading and traceability block to use US-2 identifier.

responses.response: Added explicit user story identifier US-3 to User Story 3 and corrected its traceability reference to (See US-3).
responses.what_changed: Modified User Story 3 heading and traceability block to use US-3 identifier.

responses.response: Reduced the gene set to a maximum of 5,000 most variable genes after CPM filtering (FR-003) to make pairwise correlation feasible on a GitHub Actions runner. Updated FR-003, FR-009 runtime bound justification, and the Assumptions section. Added a Phase Mapping section that includes FR-012 and FR-025, ensuring they are mapped to appropriate phases.
responses.what_changed: Edited FR-003 text, added gene‑count limit; clarified FR-009 runtime feasibility; revised Assumptions; added Phase Mapping section; added (See US-1) to FR-012 and FR-025.

new_concerns.text: FR-012 (CLI flag `--seed <int>` for reproducible random seed) is defined in the Functional Requirements but does not appear in any Phase Mapping entry, leaving its implementation phase unspecified.

FR-025 (Retention of raw correlation scores for unbiased AUROC/AUPRC) is defined in the Functional Requirements but is absent from the Phase Mapping table, so the phase responsible for generating and storing these raw scores is unclear.
